name: Urcuchillay
description: Urcuchillay, also known as the Llama deity, held a significant position in Incan mythology, particularly within the agricultural and pastoral aspects of their society. He was revered as the protector of livestock, especially llamas and alpacas, which were crucial for transportation, wool production, and sustenance in the Andean highlands. Urcuchillay was believed to control the weather, specifically rainfall and hail, which directly impacted the success of crops and the health of livestock. Farmers and herders prayed to him for favorable weather conditions to ensure bountiful harvests and thriving herds. He was often depicted as a multi-colored llama, sometimes adorned with lightning bolts, symbolizing his connection to the sky and its powerful forces. Offerings, including coca leaves and animal sacrifices, were made to Urcuchillay to appease him and secure his blessings. His influence extended beyond mere protection; he was also seen as a fertility god, ensuring the continuation of both plant and animal life. Temples and shrines dedicated to Urcuchillay were often located near fields and pastures, reflecting his close association with the land and its bounty. The importance of Urcuchillay highlights the Incan reverence for the natural world and their deep dependence on agriculture and animal husbandry for survival. His worship underscores the intricate relationship between the Incan people and their environment, emphasizing the need for harmony and respect for the forces of nature.
subtitle: Protector of Llamas and Weather God

keyMyths.title: Urcuchillay and the First Llamas
keyMyths.description: Legend says Urcuchillay created the first llamas from clay, breathing life into them to provide sustenance and transportation for the Incan people. He taught them how to care for the animals and utilize their resources wisely.

keyMyths.title: The Drought and Urcuchillay's Intervention
keyMyths.description: During a severe drought, the people pleaded with Urcuchillay for rain. Moved by their suffering, he unleashed a powerful storm, ending the drought and saving their crops and livestock.

keyMyths.title: Urcuchillay and the Mountain Spirits
keyMyths.description: Urcuchillay is said to command the mountain spirits, ensuring the balance of the ecosystem. He can summon them to protect the mountains and its inhabitants from harm.

sources.author: Maria Rostworowski de Diez Canseco
